plant.description: Unique History: Classified under the 'Vayah-sthapana' (age-stabilizing) and 'Kushthaghna' (skin-disease-destroying) groups in ancient Ayurvedic treatises like Charaka Samhita. The name 'Karanja' literally translates to 'the one that stays green'. It is spiritually associated with 'Sattva' (purity and clarity). Mentioned in classical Sanskrit literature as 'Pongame Oil Tree'. Physical Characteristics: A medium-to-large evergreen tree growing up to 15-20 meters in height, with a majestic crown and dark-grey, rough bark. The leaves are alternate, pinnate, and green. The flowers are attractive, pea-like, white or pale-purple in axillary racemes. The fruit is an oval pod containing a single reddish-brown seed. It is found throughout India and tropical/sub-tropical regions worldwide and is widely cultivated globally for medicinal and biofuel use. Specific Chemical Compounds: Primarily contains Furanoflavones (Karanjin), Chalcones, and Steroids. The presence of karanjen is crucial for its anti-viral, anti-carcinogenic, and anti-inflammatory action on the immune system. Strict Standardizations: The seed is standardized in API based on its reddish-brown color and characteristic aromatic-bitter-pungent taste. Physicochemical benchmarks: total ash (<5%), alcohol-soluble extractive (>10%), and water-soluble extractive (>15%). HPLC is used to monitor 1% to 2% Karanjin in commercial extracts.

ayurvedic_properties.dosha_karma: Used in Kustha (Skin diseases), Palitya (premature greying), Artava-dosha (menstrual disorders), Arsha (hemorrhoids), and Kamala (jaundice).

seed_oil__taila_.method: Pure seed oil taken (5-10 drops) once daily with warm milk or on a piece of bread (to mask pungent taste) for enhancing memory and intellect.

seed_powder__churna_.method: 1-3g of fine powder taken twice daily with honey or warm milk (as an Anupana) for managing mental stress and cognition.

seed_decoction__kwatha_.method: 5g of dried seeds boiled in 100ml water reduced to 25ml for chronic joint pain and edema. Take twice daily before meals.

root_paste__lepa_.method: Ground fresh root or paste applied directly to localized inflammations (Mumps, boils, joint pain) to reduce heat and swelling.

external_oil_application.method: Karanjadi Taila applied topically (mixed with sesame oil) to treat chronic skin diseases, alopecia, and non-healing ulcers.

dosage.children: Generally safe. 125 mg to 250 mg of powder mixed with honey twice daily for respiratory and digestive protection during childhood infections.
dosage.adults: Powder: 1-3 grams; Swarasa (Juice): 5-10 ml; Decoction: 20-50 ml; Oil: 5-10 drops (internal, with precautions), divided throughout the day before meals.
dosage.elderly: Ideal for age-related Vata issues and maintaining joint health. Dosage: 1g of powder daily with warm milk at bedtime.

medicinal_uses: Expert Dermatological and Skin Specialist: Karanja is the premier Ayurvedic remedy for all conditions of chronic skin diseases (Kustha) and non-healing ulcers. Its 'Tikshna' (sharp) and 'Ushna' (heating) nature provided by furanoflavonoids helps in clearing deep-seated pathogens and localized Kapha. WHO-aligned research recognizes its potent anti-microbial and cyto-protective action.

Hemorrhoids and Gut Health: Its 'Grahi' (absorbent) and 'Deepana' (appetizer) actions make it highly effective for managing burning hemorrhoids, chronic diarrhea, and inflammatory bowel conditions by strengthening the gut lining.

Anti-inflammatory and Wound Healing: Acts as a powerful agent for managing 'Vrana' (chronic sores/ulcers). Its purified paste helps in 'Vranashodhana' (purification of wounds) and 'Vranaropana' (healing), especially in cases of foul-smelling or necrotic tissue.

Respiratory and Cough support: Its Ushna (heating) potency helps in liquefying and expelling thick mucus from the lungs through its Tikshna-Katu (sharp-pungent) action. It is traditionally used in 'Kasa' (cough) and 'Shwasa' (asthma) when there is excess Kapha accumulation.

Anthelminthic and Gut Support: Its 'Krimighna' (worm-destroying) property is utilized primarily in external applications to resolve localized parasitic skin infections and internally (with extreme caution) for specific intestinal parasites.

Metabolic Support and Obesity: Helps in reducing excessive body fat (Meda) by stimulating the 'Agni' (metabolism) and inhibiting the synthesis of fatty acids. It is often combined with black pepper and long pepper (Trikatu) for enhancing metabolic efficiency.

Phytochemical Profile: Rich in Furanoflavonoids (Karanjin, Pongapin), Pinnatin, Gamatin, and various sterols. Karanjin is the primary bioactive responsible for its potent anti-microbial and anti-inflammatory action on the skin and liver cells.

safety_profile.warning: GRAS Status: Classified as a safe medicinal herb and oil with a history of use spanning millennia across various global cultures and traditional systems (Ayurveda, Unani, and Himachali medicine) without documented toxicity.
safety_profile.medical_supervision: Use under the guidance of a qualified Ayurvedic practitioner.
contraindications: Generally very safe. However, individuals with high Pitta (burning sensation, hyperacidity, bleeding disorders) should use fresh karanjen in moderation as it is Ushna (heating). Avoid during pregnancy or lactation in very large therapeutic doses except under professional guidance.
possible_side_effects: None documented at traditional therapeutic levels. Excessive use in very sensitive individuals may rarely cause mild skin cooling or stomach dryness. Discontinue if extreme bitterness causes nausea.
drug_interactions: Monitor if taken alongside synthetic anti-diabetic or anti-hypertensive medications as it may potentiate their effects. No severe adverse interactions reported in clinical literature.

plant_profile.note: AI estimated profile based on known properties
